channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan’s Rooftop Solar Expansion Reduces Dependence on Imported Fossil Fuels
item.description: Following record spikes in liquefied natural gas (LNG) prices after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, Pakistan experienced repeated electricity shortages, exacerbated by a heatwave and soaring energy cost...

item.title: Hawthorn duo take responsibility after off-season scissor lift incident
item.description: Hawthorn coach Sam Mitchell has addressed the recent resurfacing of bodycam footage showing players Dylan Moore and Connor Macdonald being arrested in the US last November. The footage, which shows th...

item.title: Government Introduces Measures to Strengthen Minority Stake Companies
item.description: The Tanzanian government has set out eight new directives to boost the performance of companies where it holds minority stakes. Announced during the Minority Interest Directors Forum 2026 (MIF 2026) i...

item.title: Judiciary Delays Publication of Reserved Judgments in South Africa
item.description: The South African judiciary has fallen behind in publishing reports on late judgments, with the most recent list covering the second term of 2025, nearly a year ago. This list, published on 9 October ...

item.title: Ontario to Allow Limited BYOB at Select Public Events This Summer
item.description: The Ontario government is considering loosening restrictions on bringing personal alcohol to certain public events this summer. Under new proposed rules, residents may be permitted to BYOB at select f...

item.title: Heavy Floods in Limpopo Disrupt Schooling and Daily Life
item.description: Limpopo province has been battered by heavy rains since the start of 2026, with Vhembe and Mopani districts experiencing particularly severe flooding. Bridges have been washed away, roads damaged, and...

item.title: Successful Separation of Conjoined Twins Marks Medical Milestone in Limpopo
item.description: In a groundbreaking medical achievement for Limpopo, conjoined identical twins born at Mankweng Tertiary Hospital have been successfully separated. The twins, who were delivered on 28 January 2026, we...

item.title: Iran confirms deaths of top security and Basij commanders in coordinated US-Israeli airstrikes
item.description: Iran has confirmed the assassinations of two of its most senior officials — Ali Larijani, the Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, and Gholamreza Soleimani, commander of the Basij param...

item.title: Gachagua faults Ruto over alleged interference in privately owned Nairobi Hospital
item.description: In this story, former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua raises strong objections to what he describes as President William Ruto’s interference with the management of Nairobi Hospital, a leading privat...

item.title: Second International Kiswahili Conference Rescheduled to July to Align with World Kiswahili Language Day
item.description: The Tanzanian government has decided to postpone the Second International Kiswahili Language Conference to July 4-7, 2026. Originally, the conference was set for April 27-30 at UNESCO headquarters in ...

item.title: Understanding the Rise of Meningitis Cases in the UK and Public Health Responses
item.description: Following two recent deaths and thirteen confirmed cases, meningitis has again become a topic of public concern in the UK. Historically, meningococcal disease was a major health issue, with around 2,5...

item.title: Kenyan man regrets trying to break up as girlfriend moves on quickly
item.description: A Kenyan man recently tried to break up with his girlfriend, telling her he no longer had feelings and wanted to end the relationship. However, his plan backfired spectacularly. The lady, named Chepko...

item.title: Ntuzuma Inmate Killed After Hostage Incident as Police Procedures Are Investigated
item.description: The family of a 22-year-old inmate who held Ntuzuma police officers hostage on Monday has not yet come forward to identify his body, which remains at the state mortuary. The deceased, reportedly conne...

item.title: Using AI to Predict March Madness Bracket Outcomes
item.description: As March Madness approaches, CNN's sports correspondent Don Riddell explored whether artificial intelligence can effectively predict the outcomes of the NCAA basketball tournament. Using ChatGPT, Ridd...

item.title: Ali Larijani: Key Iranian Politician and Nuclear Negotiator Assassinated
item.description: Ali Larijani, one of Iran’s most influential politicians, has been assassinated in a US-Israeli airstrike while visiting his daughter near Tehran. At 67, Larijani had spent decades as a key adviser to...

item.title: California Launches Policy to Restore 7.5 Million Acres to Indigenous Stewardship
item.description: California has announced a landmark policy to return stewardship of 7.5 million acres of land and coastal waters—about 7% of the state’s territory—to Indigenous tribes. This initiative, led by the Cal...

item.title: Winnie Odinga Proposed for New Deputy Party Leader Role in ODM
item.description: The Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) in Nairobi is considering amending its party constitution to create a fourth deputy leader position specifically for women, with Winnie Odinga being the top choice...

item.title: Babati Man Sentenced to Death for Killing Stepson Under Witchdoctor's Advice
item.description: In Babati District, Emmanuel Safari has had his death sentence by hanging upheld by the Court of Appeal after being convicted of murdering his one-and-a-half-year-old stepson and committing an unnatur...

item.title: Australia's CPI remains high despite RBA interest rate increase
item.description: Australia's consumer price index (CPI) sits at 3.8 per cent, making it one of the highest among OECD nations. The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) recently raised the official cash rate from 3.85 per c...

item.title: North West Municipalities Face Severe Service Delivery Failures, Says Human Rights Commission
item.description: The South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC) has revealed alarming service delivery failures across municipalities in the North West province, highlighting a crisis that infringes on residents' b...
